rulings.Case Title: JAVIER REYES VS. JANICE RODGERS et al
rulings.Calendar Matter: MOTION TO SET ASIDE DEFAULT/DEFAULT JUDGMENT/LEAVE TO DEFEND
rulings.Rulings: Matter on calendar for Wednesday, July 9, 2014, Line 1, DEFENDANT JANICE RODGERS' MOTION TO SET ASIDE DEFAULT/DEFAULT JUDGMENT/LEAVE TO DEFEND. Denied. Defendant Janice Rogers ("Rogers") has not established that the default judgment should be set aside. The evidence establishes that Rogers was served with the summons and complaint and had actual knowledge of the action. (Declaration of Janice Rogers, par. 3; Declaration of Scott Bassin, pars. 14-15.) Rogers was also served with the Requests for Entry of Default. (Bassin Declaration, par. 7, Ex. E.) The evidence also establishes that Carole Gregory, the co-administrator of the Estate, was served with the summons and complaint and therefore, the Estate was properly served. (Bassin Declaration, par. 4, Ex. C-2.) The amount of damages awarded by the default judgment does not exceed the relief requested in the complaint. Any party who contests a tentative ruling must send an email to contestdept302tr@sftc.org with a copy to all other parties by 4pm stating, without argument, the portion(s) of the tentative ruling that the party contests. The subject line of the email shall include the line number, case name and case number. The prevailing party is required to prepare a proposed order with the name and address of the prevailing party's counsel or the prevailing party if pro per in the top left of the first page of proposed order which repeats verbatim the substantive portion of the tentative ruling and must bring the proposed order to the hearing or email it to contestdept302tr@sftc.org prior to the hearing even if the motion is not opposed or the tentative ruling is not contested. =(302/EHG)

rulings.Case Title: PAUL BOSCHETTO VS. VOSS BORETA et al
rulings.Calendar Matter: DEMURRER TO 2ND AMENDED CROSS COMPLAINT
rulings.Rulings: Matter on calendar for Wednesday, July 9, 2014, Line 2, CROSS DEFENDANT THE VASO BORETA 2000 TRUST'S DEMURRER TO 2ND AMENDED CROSS COMPLAINT. Overruled. Nevada Revised Statute sec. 164.025 provides: "The trustee of a nontestamentary trust may after the death of the settlor of the trust cause to be published a notice¿AND mail a copy of the notice to known or readily ascertainable creditors." (Emphasis added.) The Statute further provides: "[a] person having a claim¿.against a settlor or the trust must file the claim within 90 days after the mailing, for those required to be mailed¿." It is a factual inquiry as to whether Cross-Plaintiffs were "known or readily ascertainable creditors" entitled to mailing of the notice. Further, it is a factual inquiry as to when/if the Trust mailed Cross-Plaintiffs proper notice. As such, the Court cannot determine on demurrer whether Cross-Plaintiffs were required to submit a claim or if they failed to timely submit a claim. Any party who contests a tentative ruling must send an email to contestdept302tr@sftc.org with a copy to all other parties by 4pm stating, without argument, the portion(s) of the tentative ruling that the party contests. The subject line of the email shall include the line number, case name and case number. The prevailing party is required to prepare a proposed order with the name and address of the prevailing party's counsel or the prevailing party if pro per in the top left of the first page of proposed order which repeats verbatim the substantive portion of the tentative ruling and must bring the proposed order to the hearing or email it to contestdept302tr@sftc.org prior to the hearing even if the motion is not opposed or the tentative ruling is not contested. =(302/EHG)

rulings.Case Title: RICHARD TOM VS. JEREMY D BLANK et al
rulings.Calendar Matter: Notice Of Motion And Motion To Compel Compliance With This Court'S Order Of Mar-31-14; Request For Terminating And/Or Evidentiary Sanctions And Monetary Sanctions In The Amount Of $4235.00;
rulings.Rulings: Matter on Calendar for Wednesday, July 09, 2014, Line 2, DEFENDANT JEREMY BLANK's Motion To Compel Compliance With This Court'S Order Of Mar-31-14; Request For Terminating And/Or Evidentiary Sanctions And Monetary Sanctions In The Amount Of $4235.00; Pro Tem Judge Francis Conway, a member of the California State Bar who meets all the requirements set forth in CRC 2.812 to serve as a temporary judge, has been assigned to hear this motion. Prior to the hearing all parties to the motion will be asked to sign a stipulation agreeing that the motion may be heard by the Pro Tem Judge. If all parties to the motion sign the stipulation, the hearing will proceed before the Judge Pro Tem who will decide the motion with the same authority as a Superior Court Judge. If a party appears by telephone, the stipulation may be signed via fax or consent to sign given by email. If not all parties to the motion sign the stipulation, the Pro Tem Judge will hold a hearing on the motion and, based on the papers submitted by the parties and the hearing, issue a report in the nature of a recommendation to the Dept. 302 Judge, who will then decide the motion. If a party does not appear at the hearing, the party will be deemed to have stipulated that the motion will be decided by the Pro Tem Judge with the same authority as a Superior Court Judge. The Pro Tem Judge has issued the following tentative ruling: MOTION GRANTED IN PART AND DENIED IN PART. ALL OBJECTIONS ARE OVERRULED AND PLAINTIFF ORDERED TO SERVE FURTHER VERIFIED COMPLETE AND STRAIGHTFORWARD RESPONSES, WITHOUT OBJECTIONS, TO FORM INTERROGATORY NOS. 2.7, 2.8, 6.4, 6.5, 9.1, 9.2, 12.1, 17.1, 50.1, 50.2, 50.3, 50.4, 50.5, AND 50.6, INCLUDING EACH AND EVERY SUBPART THEREOF. PLAINTIFF'S OBJECTIONS TO FORM INTERROGATORY NOS. 2.6, 3.6, AND 3.7 ARE SUSTAINED. MONETARY SANCTIONS OF $4,235, PAYABLE BY RICHARD TOM, ARE AWARDED TO JEREMY BLANK. FURTHER RESPONSES AND SANCTIONS PAYMENT SHALL BE SERVED ON DEFENSE COUNSEL NO LATER THAN 10 CALENDAR DAYS AFTER ENTRY OF ORDER ON THIS MOTION. Any party who contests a tentative ruling must send an email to FConway@partonsell.com with a copy to all other parties by 4pm stating, without argument, the portion(s) of the tentative ruling that the party contests. The subject line of the email shall include the line number, case name and case number. If the tentative ruling is not contested, the parties are deemed to have stipulated to the Pro Tem hearing the motion and the Pro Tem will sign an order confirming the tentative ruling. The prevailing party is required to prepare a proposed order repeating verbatim the substantive portion of the tentative ruling and must bring the proposed order to the hearing even if the motion is not opposed or the tentative ruling is not contested.=(302/JPT)

rulings.Case Title: CARLOTA DEL PORTILLO et al VS. BOARD OF ADMINISTRATION OF THE CALIFORNIA STATE et al
rulings.Calendar Matter: Notice Of Motion And Motion For Judgment On The Pleadings; Proof Of Service
rulings.Rulings: Matter on calendar for Wednesday, July 9, 2014, Line 10, PLAINTIFFS CARLOTA DEL PORTILLO, MAMIE HOW, RITA JONES, LEE SMITH, GARY TOM'S Motion For Judgment On The Pleadings. The court grants plaintiffs' motion for judgment on the pleadings. Respondent violated plaintiff's due process rights under American Federation of Labor and Congress of Industrial Organizations v. Cal. Employment Dev. Dept. (1979) 88 Cal.App.3d 811, 820 and Goldberg v. Kelly (1970) 397 U.S. 254 by reducing plaintiffs' vested pension benefits and mandating repayment prior to a hearing. The court finds that plaintiffs' rights were violated in this case, but does not issue a ruling regarding respondents' conduct with regard to other members of CalSTRS. Any party who contests a tentative ruling must send an email to contestdept302tr@sftc.org with a copy to all other parties by 4pm stating, without argument, the portion(s) of the tentative ruling that the party contests. The subject line of the email shall include the line number, case name and case number. rulings.Case Title: ANNE H LEE VS. SHACUAN SHARON SETO AKA SHARON SETO et al
rulings.Calendar Matter: DEMURRER TO COMPLAINT
rulings.Rulings: Matter on calendar for Wednesday, July 9, 2014, Line 11, DEFENDANTS SHACUAN SHARON SETO AKA SHARON SETO, DAVID SETO, GORDON LEE, RAISSI REAL ESTATE DEVELOPMENT, LLC, WINCHESTER PLAZA ON THE ROWE LLC, GREENLIGHT CAPITAL GROUP, LLC'S DEMURRER TO COMPLAINT. Sustained in part and overruled in part. The Demurrer is sustained for failure to state a claim as to the first, third, fourth and sixth causes of action with fifteen days leave to amend. Plaintiff must allege with the requisite specificity how and when the false representations were made, and how Defendants knew the representations were false. Plaintiff must also make factual allegations establishing that Defendants owed fiduciary duties to Plaintiff. The Demurrer is overruled as to the second and fifth causes of action for rescission as Defendants fail to make any argument to support their conclusory subject heading. Demurrer is also overruled for all causes of action on uncertainty grounds. (See Fenton v. Groveland Comm. Services Dist. (1982) 135 Cal.App.3d 797, 809 (A demurrer made on uncertainty grounds must distinctly specify exactly how or why the pleading is uncertain, and where such uncertainty appears by reference to page and line numbers in the complaint.) Any party who contests a tentative ruling must send an email to contestdept302tr@sftc.org with a copy to all other parties by 4:00 p.m. stating, without argument, the portion(s) of the tentative ruling that the party contests. The subject line of the email shall include the line number, case name and case number. The prevailing party is required to prepare a proposed order with the name and address of the prevailing party's counsel or the prevailing party if in pro per on the top left of the first page of proposed order which repeats verbatim the substantive portion of the tentative ruling and must bring the proposed order to the hearing or email it to contestdept302tr@sftc.org prior to the hearing even if the motion is not opposed or the tentative ruling is not contested. =(302/EHG)

rulings.Case Title: FRANK BROWN VS. MAUREEN BARBARA KENNEY et al
rulings.Calendar Matter: AMENDED DEMURRER TO COMPLAINT
rulings.Rulings: Matter on calendar for Wednesday, July 9, 2014, Line 12, DEFENDANT EVEREST MWAMBA'S AMENDED DEMURRER TO COMPLAINT. Sustained with 20 days leave to amend. It is unclear how the filing of the petition of separation transferred Defendant Maureen Barbara Kenney's ("Kenney") community property interest in the property listed in paragraph 8 of the complaint. The petition of separation, attached as Exhibit A to the complaint, states at paragraph 4 that it is "to be determined" what assets and debts are to be confirmed as separate property. Plaintiff is given leave to amend to allege facts illustrating how the filing of the petition of separation transferred Kenney's community property rights. Additionally, Plaintiff is given leave to amend to comply with the requirements of Cal. Rule of Court 2.112. Any party who contests a tentative ruling must send an email to contestdept302tr@sftc.org with a copy to all other parties by 4:00 p.m. stating, without argument, the portion(s) of the tentative ruling that the party contests. The subject line of the email shall include the line number, case name and case number. The prevailing party is required to prepare a proposed order with the name and address of the prevailing party's counsel or the prevailing party if in pro per on the top left of the first page of proposed order which repeats verbatim the substantive portion of the tentative ruling and must bring the proposed order to the hearing or email it to contestdept302tr@sftc.org prior to the hearing even if the motion is not opposed or the tentative ruling is not contested. =(302/EHG)
